slides.title: The Calculus of Change
slides.notes: Title slide — set the stage for a journey through calculus concepts.

children.content: A visual journey through the language of change

slides.title: Functions: The Building Blocks
slides.notes: Introduce f(x) = x² — the simplest polynomial. Show how the curve emerges from plotting input→output pairs.

slides.title: The Derivative: Instantaneous Rate of Change
slides.notes: Show f(x) = x² and its derivative f'(x) = 2x overlaid. The derivative tells us the slope at every point.

slides.title: Trigonometric Functions
slides.notes: Show sin and cos on the same axes — these oscillating functions are everywhere in nature: waves, orbits, sound.

children.content: The derivative of sin is cos — they are intimately linked

slides.title: Vectors: Direction and Magnitude
slides.notes: Vectors represent quantities with both direction and magnitude. Show vector addition visually — the parallelogram law.

slides.title: Vector Fields: Flow and Force
slides.notes: A vector field assigns a vector to every point in space. This one shows rotation — imagine fluid swirling around the origin.

slides.title: Matrices: Linear Transformations
slides.notes: A 2x2 matrix represents a linear transformation of the plane. Show the rotation matrix and how it maps basis vectors.

slides.title: Graph Theory: Networks
slides.notes: Graphs model relationships. Show a small network with weighted edges — could represent cities, neurons, or social connections.

children.content: Weighted directed graph — edges carry capacity or cost

slides.title: Euler's Identity: The Most Beautiful Equation
slides.notes: Euler's identity connects five fundamental constants: e, i, π, 1, and 0. It's often called the most beautiful equation in mathematics.

slides.title: Thank You
slides.notes: Closing slide — invite questions and further exploration.

children.expression: \text{Mathematics is the language of the universe}

children.content: Built with Elucim — animated explanations for the web
